facts: Headquartered in Alpharetta, GA.
Reported earnings results for the three months ended March 31, 2024.
Completed the sale of its Engineered Papers business on November 30, 2023.
Q1 2024 sales were $500.2 million, increasing 10.6% sequentially versus Q4 2023 and decreasing 8.9% versus the prior year.
GAAP loss was $(28.0) million with a GAAP EPS of $(0.52), including organizational realignment, integration and purchase accounting expenses.
Adjusted income was $1.1 million, Adjusted EPS was $0.02, and Adjusted EBITDA was $45.8 million.
Adjusted EBITDA was down 6% versus the prior year as impacts from lower volumes and higher production costs more than offset net benefits of input costs/selling price and synergies realization.
CEO Julie Schertell commented that meaningful sequential volume improvement occurred in Q1 and expects to deliver meaningfully improved EBITDA year-over-year for the remainder of 2024.
Initiated an organizational restructuring initiative expected to reduce non-operating costs by up to $20 million annually as they exit 2024.
Actions taken in 2023 included divesting Engineered Papers, consolidating footprint, and investing in new assets in filtration and release liners.
Filtration & Advanced Materials (FAM) segment sales were $202.7 million, up 11% sequentially versus Q4 2023 and down 8% versus the prior year period.
FAM products include filtration media and components, advanced films, coating and converting solutions, and extruded mesh products.
GAAP Operating Profit in FAM included $3.2 million of restructuring, impairment, and other expenses primarily related to organizational realignment and footprint rationalization.
Sustainable & Adhesive Solutions (SAS) segment sales were $297.5 million, up 10% sequentially versus Q4 2023 and down 9% versus the prior year period.
SAS products include tapes, labels, liners, specialty paper, packaging and healthcare solutions.
GAAP Operating Profit in SAS included $7.6 million in restructuring, impairment, and other expenses primarily related to organizational realignment and footprint rationalization.

Adjusted unallocated expenses were in line with the prior year, while GAAP operating expenses included $1.5 million of EP divestiture expenses, $3.1 million in restructuring/impairment expenses, and $4.4 million related to organizational realignment.
Interest expense was $18.3 million versus $15.6 million in the prior year period, primarily due to higher average interest rates and higher average balances on floating debt.
Tax rate was 7.9% during Q1 2024, driven by one-time tax adjustments; excluding these, the rate was 16.8%.
Year-to-date 2024 cash used in operating activities was $13.0 million, with capital spending and software costs totaling $12.6 million.
Total debt was $1,158.0 million as of March 31, 2024, with total cash of $128.9 million resulting in net debt of $1,029.1 million.
Total liquidity was approximately $409 million, consisting of $129 million of cash and $280 million of revolver availability.
Company's debt matures on a staggered basis between 2026 and 2028.
Announced next quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share payable on June 21, 2024 to stockholders of record as of May 24, 2024.
Did not repurchase shares during the first quarter but intends to periodically repurchase shares to offset dilution due to stock compensation.
Will hold a conference call to review Q1 2024 results at 8:30 a.m. Eastern time on Thursday, May 9, 2024.
Mativ is a global leader in specialty materials targeting premium applications across diversified end-markets from filtration to healthcare to sustainable packaging.
